origin.name: Germany
origin.death_registration: Local Standesamt (registry office) within 3 working days
origin.leichenpass_note: The Leichenpass is Germany's international body-transport document. Required for all repatriations. Adds approximately 1-2 days.
origin.key_document: Sterbeurkunde (death certificate from Standesamt) and Leichenpass
origin.source: FCDO Germany travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Berlin guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom

airlines.name: Lufthansa Cargo
airlines.routes: FRA, MUC, BER, DUS to LHR, LGW, MAN, BHX, EDI

special_rules: Death must be registered at the local Standesamt within 3 working days.
Leichenpass required for all international repatriations. Adds approximately 1-2 days.
Germany has 16 federal states each with its own funeral law, but the broad process is consistent.
Post-mortem (Gerichtsmedizin) ordered for violent, suspicious, or unexplained deaths. Adds weeks.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All German documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Berlin and consulates in Munich and Dusseldorf can register the death and advise. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Germany to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Germany. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days. Standesamt process, Leichenpass, and Lufthansa Cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
special_rules: Irish Embassy in Berlin handles consular matters.
Leichenpass required identically to UK repatriations.
All documents require certified English translation.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All German documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Berlin can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Germany to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Germany and needs to come home to Ireland. Repatriation takes 7-14 days. Standesamt and Leichenpass process all handled. Contact us 24/7.
